meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 2)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Practice choosing the correct singular or plural noun in sentences.

questions.question: What is the plural of "bear"?
questions.explanation: bear → bears. Regular nouns just add -s.

questions.question: "Apple" is a singular noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "apple" means one apple — that makes it singular.

questions.question: My friend and I have two ___. (bag)
questions.explanation: "two" tells us we need a plural. bag → bags.

questions.question: The plural of "dish" is "dishes".
questions.explanation: Yes! dish ends in -sh, so we add -es: dish → dishes.

questions.question: Write the plural of "kite".
questions.explanation: kite → kites. Regular nouns just add -s.

questions.question: Ana sees some birds on the tree ___. (branch)
questions.explanation: branch → branches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Write the plural of "fox".
questions.explanation: fox → foxes. Words ending in -x add -es.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: dog/fox/tree = one (singular). watches/dishes = more than one (plural).

questions.question: In the sentence "Luke and Ella ride their bikes home," the word "bikes" is a plural noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "bikes" is the plural of "bike". Luke AND Ella each have a bike.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Six bugs are on the rocks."

questions.question: Which noun follows the rule 'ending in -ss → add -es'?
questions.explanation: class ends in -ss, so its plural is classes (add -es).

questions.question: Write the plural of "star".
questions.explanation: star → stars. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -x or -s add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I open my books to page 8.
I open my book to page 8.
I open my bookes to page 8.
I open my bookies to page 8.
questions.explanation: "I open my book" — this is one book, so we use the singular. No number clue says plural.

questions.question: The plural of "class" is "classes".
questions.explanation: Yes! class ends in -ss, so we add -es: class → classes.

questions.question: Write the plural of "beach".
questions.explanation: beach → beaches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "I plant two bushes in the yard."

questions.question: What is the plural of "ostrich"?
questions.explanation: ostrich ends in -ch, so we add -es: ostrich → ostriches.

questions.question: In the sentence "One frog eats the bugs," the noun "frog" is plural.
questions.explanation: "One frog" means just one frog — that's singular, not plural.

questions.question: We see many ___ in the city. (church)
questions.explanation: church → churches. Words ending in -ch add -es. "many" tells us we need a plural.
